title: calceolaria ascendens subsp. exigua
intro: Calceolaria ascendens subsp. exigua, also known as the "small-pouched slipper flower" or "small slipper flower," is a plant species belonging to the Calceolaria genus, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and delicate appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The medicinal properties of Calceolaria ascendens subsp. exigua are believed to provide various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
